title: USING PREFIXES TO CONTROL FLOATING VIRGIN OPERATIONS
abstract: Setting or updating of floating point controls is managed. Floating point controls include controls used for floating point operations, such as rounding mode and/or other controls. Further, floating point controls include status associated with floating point operations, such as floating point exceptions and/or others. The management of the floating point controls includes efficiently updating the controls, while reducing costs associated therewith.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer program product for facilitating processing within a computing environment, the computer program product comprising: a computer readable storage medium readable by a processing circuit and storing instructions for performing a method comprising: employing a control prefix to provide a user-specified value for a floating point control for a selected instruction to be processed; and executing the selected instruction using the user-specified value for the floating point control specified by the control prefix, the control prefix overriding another value of the floating point control provided by a centralized status control. 2. The computer program product of claim 1 , wherein the control prefix is included with the selected instruction. 3. The computer program product of claim 1, wherein the control prefix is a prefix instruction added to an instruction stream prior to the selected instruction. 4. The computer program product of claim 3, wherein the prefix instruction comprises at least one control field indicating at least one floating point control to be provided by the prefix instruction. 5. The computer program product of claim 4, wherein the prefix instruction further comprises at least one control field value indicating at least one value for the at least one floating point control. 6. The computer program product of claim 4, wherein the prefix instruction further includes an indicator to specify whether a control field of the at least one control field is enabled. 7. The computer program product of claim 1 , wherein the floating point control comprises a rounding mode. 8. The computer program product of claim 1 , wherein the floating point control comprises a floating point exception. 9. The computer program product of claim 1 , wherein the centralized status control comprises a floating point control register. 10. The computer program product of claim 1 , wherein the method further comprises: determining whether the floating point control is a selected control; determining, based on the floating point control being the selected control, which type of floating point instruction the selected control is to be applied, wherein there is a plurality of types of floating point instructions including at least one of binary, decimal, hexadecimal and vector; and applying the floating point control to the determined type of floating point instruction. 1 1. A computer system for facilitating processing within a computing environment, the computer system comprising: a memory; and a processor in communication with the memory, wherein the computer system is configured to perform a method, said method comprising: employing a control prefix to provide a user-specified value for a floating point control for a selected instruction to be processed; and executing the selected instruction using the user-specified value for the floating point control specified by the control prefix, the control prefix overriding another value of the floating point control provided by a centralized status control. 12. The computer system of claim 1 1 , wherein the control prefix is included with the selected instruction. 13. The computer system of claim 1 1 , wherein the control prefix is a prefix instruction added to an instruction stream prior to the selected instruction. 14. The computer system of claim 1 1 , wherein the floating point control comprises a rounding mode. 15. The computer system of claim 1 1 , wherein the method further comprises: determining whether the floating point control is a selected control; determining, based on the floating point control being the selected control, which type of floating point instruction the selected control is to be applied, wherein there is a plurality of types of floating point instructions including at least one of binary, decimal, hexadecimal and vector; and applying the floating point control to the determined type of floating point instruction. 16. A computer-implemented method of facilitating processing within a computing environment, the computer- implemented method comprising: employing, by a processor, a control prefix to provide a user-specified value for a floating point control for a selected instruction to be processed; and 

 executing the selected instruction using the user-specified value for the floating point control specified by the control prefix, the control prefix overriding another value of the floating point control provided by a centralized status control. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the control prefix is included with the selected instruction. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the control prefix is a prefix instruction added to an instruction stream prior to the selected instruction. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 18, wherein the prefix instruction comprises at least one control field indicating at least one floating point control to be provided by the prefix instruction. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein the prefix instruction further comprises at least one control field value indicating at least one value for the at least one floating point control. 21. The computer-implemented method of claim 19, wherein the prefix instruction further includes an indicator to specify whether a control field of the at least one control field is enabled. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the floating point control comprises a rounding mode. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the floating point control comprises a floating point exception. 24. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the centralized status control comprises a floating point control register. 25. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, further comprising: determining whether the floating point control is a selected control; determining, based on the floating point control being the selected control, which type of floating point instruction the selected control is to be applied, wherein there is a plurality of types of floating point instructions including at least one of binary, decimal, hexadecimal and vector; and applying the floating point control to the determined type of floating point instruction.
